rules.removalPolicy: Only remove entries when Michael explicitly asks Codex to remove them.

characters.name: The Fool

image.prompt: Use the supplied concept art as the primary visual anchor for The Fool: a serious mythic fantasy traveler, not a jester, wearing a dark wide-brim hat, loose scarf covering the lower face, weathered black long coat with burgundy lining and trim, bracers, boots, waist belt, sword and scabbard, hidden satchel, tattered hem, solemn eyes, protective presence, and quiet moral clarity.

characters.biography: The Fool is the hidden heart of the story, called a fool by broken kingdoms because they no longer recognize honorable manhood. He does not perform, boast, or control how others judge him. He acts, bears burdens, tells the truth plainly, keeps faith, and walks into darkness when protection is required.

biographyLog.text: The Fool is the hidden heart of the story, called a fool by broken kingdoms because they no longer recognize honorable manhood. He does not perform, boast, or control how others judge him. He acts, bears burdens, tells the truth plainly, keeps faith, and walks into darkness when protection is required.

physical.clothing: eventual ensemble uses black and red with white trims; initial concept art shows a dark wide-brim hat, loose scarf, weathered black coat, burgundy inner lining/trim, bracers, boots, waist belt, sword frog, scabbard attachment, and hidden inner satchel
physical.facialHair: not locked; lower face is concealed by scarf in initial concept art; use male facial hair reference guide if a visible style is needed
physical.distinguishingFeatures: not a clown, not a jester, not comic relief; silhouette reads as solemn wanderer/guardian
physical.scaleNotes: Needs believable physical presence for protection, labor, endurance, and ship/crew authority without looking like vanity-power fantasy.

physicalNotes.text: Physical properties are intentionally not fully locked yet. Preserve the serious, weathered, burden-bearing presence.

characters.politics: He sees outside the ideological circles of corrupt kingdoms. His difference should be shown through story rather than explained as a lecture.

relationships.name: Gwenevere
relationships.details: The Fool and Gwenevere are the hidden heart of the story. Their union is the answer to the kingdoms.

relationships.name: The Smuggler
relationships.details: One of the true good men; keeps hidden roads open for the innocent.

relationships.name: The Pillar
relationships.details: The steady man who remains, repairs, carries, and holds things up.

visualReferences.title: Initial concept art
visualReferences.notes: Primary concept sheet with front, back, and three-quarter views plus details for hat, loose scarf, coat trim and tattered hem, waist belt, sword frog, sword hilt and scabbard attachment, bracers, boots, and hidden inner satchel.

aiPrompts.text: Write The Fool as serious, mythic, symbolic, emotionally weighty, and morally charged. Do not make him a clown, jester, quippy antihero, or generic fantasy protagonist. He is quietly formidable because he bears truth and duty, not because he performs dominance.

importantDetails.text: The Fool is not formally titled Fool. Corrupt kingdoms call him that because they misread him.
His eventual Cap'n identity should emerge through trials, crew respect, and fatherly/manly competence.
His joining or relation to the Last Ward should feel like conversion and formation, analogous to being brought into the fold to learn how to live rightly.

Initial concept art added as the primary visual anchor. Preserve the serious masked wanderer look: hat, scarf, long weathered black coat, burgundy lining, bracers, boots, belt, sword, and hidden satchel.
Portrait crop added from the initial concept sheet so the character page can show a single-character portrait first, with the full concept sheet below as reference art.

crossReferences.label: The Last Ward
crossReferences.note: Origin, formation, and moral homeland reference.

characters.name: Gwenevere

image.prompt: Use Gwenevere Target 20260507 as the locked visual anchor: adult young woman, fair freckled skin, soft green eyes, wounded but composed expression, long dark brown wavy hair with loose side braid, aqua/teal/seafoam and soft rose showpiece gown, delicate gold details, Hall clasp, hidden Mercy pouch, beautiful but not cherished, not noble, not a queen.

characters.biography: Gwenevere is the Maiden at the beginning of the story: beautiful, kind, publicly displayed, and privately unseen. She is not noble, not cherished, not a shopwife, and not a queen. Her beginning design should communicate that she is shown off by a vain kingdom but not truly protected or cared for.

biographyLog.text: Initial Gwenevere character entry created from the supplied intro concept sheet and square portrait reference.
Gwenevere Reference Art V1 received and set as the current locked visual source for the webpage and character generator.
Gwenevere Target 20260507 received and set as the current locked visual target for character-generator identity and outfit reference.

physical.build: soft mature feminine adult presence; graceful natural proportions; gentle physical softness
physical.hair: locked/active direction from Reference Art V1: dark brown wavy hair, loose side braid over one shoulder, natural stray strands around the face, lived-in and stressed rather than salon-perfect; avoid inflated hair volume.
physical.skin: fair with natural freckles and human texture
physical.clothing: locked intro palette: aqua, teal, seafoam, soft pink, silver, and gold. Showpiece ceremonial gown with bare shoulders, Hall clasp, hidden Mercy pouch near heart, fine fabric chosen for display rather than care.
physical.distinguishingFeatures: wounded but composed expression; not noble, not cherished, not a queen; beautiful but not well cared for; her colors are hers but the meaning of the dress is imposed on her
physical.scaleNotes: All character images for the dashboard should be square-framed. Full-body images may be generated tall and then square-padded if needed, but the final saved repository asset should be square.

physicalNotes.text: Visual design should match the supplied Gwenevere intro reference exactly unless Michael explicitly changes the lock.
Visual lock updated to Gwenevere Reference Art V1. Use the full sheet for canon review and the derived face/full-body square crops for image generation guidance.

characters.politics: Her public display exposes the moral sickness of the Hall: she is possessed, ornamented, and used as proof of status rather than protected as a person.

relationships.name: The Fool
relationships.details: The Fool and Gwenevere are the hidden heart of the story. Their union is the answer to the kingdoms.

visualReferences.title: ComfyUI square reference-guided test

visualReferences.title: Full-body square reference crop
visualReferences.notes: Retouched square crop from the supplied concept sheet for rough full-body/costume reference. Not a final generated portrait.

visualReferences.title: ComfyUI full-body img2img test
visualReferences.notes: Square test image from the full-body reference crop. Useful for composition/costume direction, not canon-locked because face and finish drift from the locked reference.

visualReferences.title: ComfyUI PhotoMaker full-body test
visualReferences.notes: Square-padded PhotoMaker test. Useful proof that long-shot composition can work, but not canon-locked because hair, dress, and face drift from the locked Gwenevere design.

visualReferences.title: ComfyUI PhotoMaker child-rescue test
visualReferences.notes: Square-padded test for Gwenevere carrying/protecting a child. Composition is useful, but it is not canon-locked because the outfit and face are not close enough to the locked reference.

visualReferences.title: Character-lock standing test
visualReferences.notes: PhotoMaker plus IPAdapter face/outfit plus OpenPose. Anatomy and full-body pose are improved; still not canon-quality because face, hair, and proportions drift from the locked reference.

visualReferences.title: Character-lock identity sweep test
visualReferences.notes: Parameter sweep with stronger face lock. Better full-body, side braid, and no extra feet; still too far from the locked Gwenevere face and setting.

aiPrompts.text: Generate Gwenevere as the same adult woman from the locked reference: fair freckled skin, soft green eyes, messy brunette hair with loose side braid, vulnerable wounded expression, aqua/teal/seafoam/soft pink showpiece dress, Hall clasp, hidden Mercy pouch. She is displayed but not loved, claimed but not protected, beautiful but not cherished. Keep images square-framed unless another format is explicitly requested.

importantDetails.text: At a glance: not noble, not cherished, a showpiece, not a queen, beautiful but not well cared for, owned in public and unseen in private, her colors are hers.

Key design notes: wears what she wants in color, wears what he wants in meaning, the Hall clasp shows his claim, the Mercy pouch near her heart holds what he does not understand, she is his to display but not hers to rely on.

Detail callouts: one Hall clasp as Ryker's claim, hidden Mercy pouch near heart, longer front slit, bare shoulders, showpiece neckline, unkept details in hair/skin/posture, not noble and not pampered.

Canon export received May 6, 2026 and copied to book/reference/gwenevere-canon-export.txt. The file appears truncated at the beginning of Clothing and Visual Tone, ending at "* a p"; loaded canon through core identity, symbolism, personality, physical appearance, body design, and hair canon.

ComfyUI tuning note: PhotoMaker model photomaker-v1.bin was installed for identity consistency. It preserves face better than plain SDXL/img2img, but full-body and child-rescue scenes still need pose control, likely ControlNet/OpenPose, before they should be treated as canon-quality.

Reusable character pipeline added: PhotoMaker for identity, IPAdapter face/outfit reference for visual consistency, and OpenPose ControlNet for pose/anatomy. This is the correct direction, but Gwenevere still needs either a cleaner full-body reference sheet or a trained character LoRA before outputs should be treated as exact/canon.

politics.name: Ortharchy
politics.biography: Ortharchy means rule under right order. It is not normal monarchy: monarchy asks who rules, Ortharchy asks what rule is bound to.

properties.emergencyRefusalLaw: No oath binds a man to disorder

importantDetails.text: The Crown Warden cannot make evil good by decree, cannot use crystals, cannot command innocent death, and cannot dissolve family, duty, protection, or truth for convenience.

politics.name: The Proven Crown
politics.biography: The Proven Crown is the common government name and crown system of The Last Ward. The ruler is chosen from the proven through the Wardmoot and The Proving, then bound by The Burden Oath.

importantDetails.text: The crown is weight, not glory.

rulers.name: Crown Warden
rulers.biography: The Crown Warden is the head of state and final executive burden of The Last Ward. He commands in war, judges in great crisis, guards the Mercies if known or entrusted, protects the Ward from invasion and breach, and cannot rule against right order.

properties.restraint: right order, oath, office, tradition, lawful refusal, and the Wardmoot

importantDetails.text: The Crown Warden bears final responsibility but does not own the order.

realms.name: The Last Ward

image.prompt: A fortified remnant country called The Last Ward: white stone walls, red banners, black ironwork, warm lantern streets, family courts, training yards, chapels or holy halls, terraced gardens, fortified gates, water channels, granaries like fortresses, disciplined defenders, peaceful because it is defended.

realms.biography: The Last Ward is a fortified remnant state of roughly 850,000 Wardborn, governed by Ortharchy through The Proven Crown. It is ordered under truth, oath, mercy, family, protected life, disciplined force, and right order. It is the last ordered remnant against collapse, large enough to be a real country with cities, farms, roads, families, military depth, trade, provinces, and pressure from the horrors.

properties.landArea: 32,000 square miles, flexible range 25,000 to 45,000 square miles
properties.corePhrase: A house worth defending in a world teaching itself how to die

sections.title: Core Identity
sections.body: The Last Ward should remain disciplined, burdened, and under pressure, but not helpless or village-sized. Its canon identity is that it resists horrors better because it still understands truth, burden, stewardship, mercy, oath, right order, and the house as something worth defending.

items.label: Civilizational role
items.value: the last ordered remnant against collapse

items.label: Moral center
items.value: truth, mercy, burden, oath, ordered force, family, protection, and keeping

items.label: Core phrase
items.value: A house worth defending in a world teaching itself how to die

sections.title: Political Structure: Ortharchy
sections.body: Ortharchy is not normal monarchy. A monarchy answers who rules. Ortharchy answers what rule is bound to. The Crown Warden wears the crown, but the crown is bound beneath right order. He cannot make evil good by decree, use crystals, command the destruction of innocent life, or dissolve family, duty, protection, or truth for convenience.

items.label: Emergency refusal law
items.value: No oath binds a man to disorder

items.label: Main restraint on ruler
items.value: right order, oath, office, tradition, lawful refusal, and the Wardmoot

Carries final responsibility when lesser offices fail

sections.title: Population and Scale
sections.body: The Ward is pressured but not helpless. It can field armies, defend cities, absorb refugees, and maintain farms, roads, fortresses, and trade.

entries.name: Active defense population included inside total

sections.title: Capital: The Last Hold
sections.body: The Last Hold should feel beautiful and coveted, but not soft. It is peaceful because it is defended.

sections.title: Land and Geography
sections.body: Recommended land area is 32,000 square miles, flexible between 25,000 and 45,000 square miles. This is large enough for multiple provinces and self-feeding depth, small enough to remain a remnant state, and defensible through terrain.

sections.title: Military and Defense
sections.body: Primary doctrine: hold, shield, rescue, purge, endure. The Ward does not worship force and does not deny force; it orders force toward protection.

sections.title: Defense Doctrine and Monster Readiness
list: never rely on Woundsalt or crystals
Most common threats near the Ward: T1 common outer leaks, T2 road hunters and field stalkers, T3 settlement level danger, T4 major local emergency, T5 breach expansion emergency

A T5 is a major alarm because that class begins widening, stabilizing, or preparing breaches for greater entry. Higher classes require wider and more stable openings, and T10 cannot fully emerge without a major wound in reality.

sections.title: Military Culture
list: Force is for defense and restraint
The weak are not proof of superiority
The strong are not permitted to become predators
A sword exists to guard the hearth
A father is not a tyrant
A mother is not a servant
The crown is weight, not glory

sections.title: Economy
sections.body: Household centered mixed economy under moral law. It is not socialism, communism, pure capitalism, merchant oligarchy, state ownership of everything, or a free appetite market.
list: iron, stone, timber, wool, leather, medicine

entries.name: Forbidden imports
list: spell instruments powered by corrupt crystal craft

sections.title: Religion and Moral Order
sections.body: The Last Ward should feel Orthodox Christian rooted without directly naming Christianity.
list: Force must be restrained by truth

items.label: Core unit
items.value: family under oath, duty, love, and protection

items.label: Marriage meaning
items.value: force covenant, fertility trust, inheritance bond, and household keeping

items.label: Fatherhood
items.value: guardianship, final burden, restraint, provision, protection

items.label: Motherhood
items.value: life keeping, nurture, formation, mercy, household wisdom

items.label: Children
items.value: entrusted life, not property, not trophies, not burdensome calculations

items.label: Widows and orphans
items.value: protected by law and household networks

items.label: Refugees
items.value: received under probation, care, and formation

entries.name: Primary education
list: care for the young and old

sections.title: Cultural Colors and Architecture
sections.body: White belongs to mercy, purity, walls, and holy order. Red belongs to sacrifice, courage, blood rightly spent, and defense. Black belongs to restraint, mourning, iron, and sober duty. Beauty is allowed because it is defended.

sections.title: Laws
list: No crystal craft may be used.
No Woundsalt may be traded for use.
No innocent life may be intentionally destroyed.
No father may abandon household burden without judgment.
No mother may be stripped of household honor by appetite law.
No oath binds a man to evil.
No household may refuse lawful defense duty.
No child may be treated as trophy, property, or inconvenience.
No medicine may conceal harm while pretending to heal.

entries.name: Natural allies
list: old houses that reject crystal use
some White Pill or restoration aligned people if purified from false hope
families fleeing Ivory Bastion or Hall corruption
honest traders who accept Ward law

sections.title: Technology and Military Equipment
sections.body: Base level is late medieval to early renaissance fantasy. The Ward has better metallurgy, wall engineering, grain storage, water systems, road defense, anti-horror equipment, discipline, training, medical ethics, and logistics.

entries.name: Not allowed
list: casual fantasy firearms unless later approved

entries.name: Common arms
list: lamellar or scale in border regions

entries.name: Internal weaknesses
list: older families becoming proud of being the good realm
young people tempted by outer glamour
pressure to use crystals just once
danger of Ortharchy becoming status pride if virtue decays

entries.name: External pressures
list: The Ivory Bastion wants to expose it as oppressive.
The Hall of Kings wants to mock it as weak or outdated.
Veymark may want access to its roads, purity reputation, or defensive craft.
Refugees strain its food and formation systems.
The Last Ward must stay generous without becoming naive.

sections.title: Story Function
sections.body: The Last Ward is not the place where the story becomes easy. It is the place that proves another order is possible. Its goodness creates pressure because people come for shelter, reputation cleansing, safety, beauty, or real conversion. Some truly change. Some only hide inside its goodness.

items.label: Moral identity
items.value: right order, mercy, burden, family, protected life, and disciplined force

aiPrompts.text: Use The Last Ward as the red-white-black fortified remnant of right order: beautiful because defended, merciful without indulgence, strong without predation, Orthodox-rooted without naming Christianity directly, disciplined under The Proven Crown and the Law of Right Order.

importantDetails.text: Soft locked: population 850,000, capital 115,000, government Ortharchy, common crown name The Proven Crown, ruler Crown Warden, land area 32,000 square miles, 9 provinces, 8 to 10 major cities, 50,000 defense capable population, primary colors red/white/black accents.
The Last Ward must be a real country with cities, farms, roads, military depth, families, trade, and provinces. It should not feel like a tiny village.
The Ward is good but not perfect. Its goodness creates pressure, envy, refugee strain, temptation, fatigue, and the risk that older families become proud of being the good realm.

crossReferences.label: The Fool
crossReferences.note: Main character with formation and origin tied to the Last Ward.

crossReferences.label: The Last Hold
crossReferences.note: Capital fortress city and seat of the Proven Crown.

crossReferences.label: Redwall Ford
crossReferences.note: River fortress city controlling the main crossing.

crossReferences.label: Highmere Watch
crossReferences.note: Mountain fortress town for pass defense and monster watch.

crossReferences.label: Saint's Crossing
crossReferences.note: Road, rescue, caravan, scout, and messenger town.

crossReferences.label: Fisherward
crossReferences.note: Water, fish, salt, boat, trade, and patrol city.

crossReferences.label: Stoneacre
crossReferences.note: Quarry and craft town for walls, roads, ironwork, and siege materials.

crossReferences.label: Mercyfield
crossReferences.note: Protected farming and family province center.

crossReferences.label: Ashgate March
crossReferences.note: Border march town and early shield against outer pressure.

crossReferences.label: Crown Warden
crossReferences.note: Ruler title and final executive burden under right order.

crossReferences.label: Ortharchy
crossReferences.note: Government principle: rule under right order.

crossReferences.label: The Proven Crown
crossReferences.note: Common government and crown system of the Last Ward.

crossReferences.label: The Wardmoot
crossReferences.note: Choosing and recognition body for the Proven Crown.

crossReferences.label: The Lawkeepers
crossReferences.note: Courts, disputes, inheritance, family law, crimes, and oath matters.

crossReferences.label: The Wallwardens
crossReferences.note: Walls, roads, passes, gates, and border keeps.

crossReferences.label: The Hearthwardens
crossReferences.note: Households, food security, children, marriage, inheritance, and domestic stability.

crossReferences.label: The Lantern Orders
crossReferences.note: Care, medicine, refuge, recovery, mercy, and grief tending.

crossReferences.label: The Grayknights
crossReferences.note: Heavy defense, breach response, and high-risk monster pressure.

crossReferences.label: Saints
crossReferences.note: Elite speed, rescue, terrain, discipline, and precision defenders.

crossReferences.label: Paladins
crossReferences.note: Elite armored defenders for direct confrontation and last stands.

map.note: Soft placeholder location for The Last Ward until the final map is supplied.

map.notes: ComfyUI parchment map test. Use as a temporary interactive map background while the true world geography is developed. Pins and travel estimates are handled by the local dashboard.
